# FJI tropical cyclone — `fji-storms`

The Fiji National Anticipatory Action Framework for Tropical Cyclones (Phase 2, 2025–2027) activates pre-arranged financing and humanitarian action when RSMC Nadi forecasts a tropical system whose wind field is expected to expose at least 5,000 people to ≥ 64-knot winds (readiness: within the 120-hour forecast; action: within the 72-hour forecast), OR when IMERG satellite rainfall data observes ≥ 190 mm average over two consecutive days across Fiji while a tropical cyclone centre is within 250 km. On activation, CERF pre-arranged funds (~USD 2.4M), agency co-financing, and IFRC DREF flow to clusters for cash transfers, protection kits, health/WASH supplies, shelter kits, and early warning messaging. NDRMO leads; FMS provides the operational forecast; OCHA CHD operates the automated trigger monitoring pipeline.

**Current version:** [2025-12-17](2025-12-17.md) · status: **endorsed** · repo `ocha-dap/pa-aa-fji-storms`
